rubrics.name: Clarity & Communication
rubrics.description: How easy is it to understand what the author is saying? Are ideas expressed without unnecessary ambiguity?

scoring_guide.0-3: Confusing, hard to follow, reader regularly loses the thread
scoring_guide.4-6: Mostly understandable but some passages require re-reading
scoring_guide.7-10: Clear throughout, reader always knows what is being said and why

rubrics.name: Structure & Organization
rubrics.description: Does the piece have a logical flow? Is there a clear beginning, middle, and end? Do sections connect to each other?

scoring_guide.0-3: No discernible structure, ideas appear in random order
scoring_guide.4-6: Some structure present but transitions are weak or ordering is questionable
scoring_guide.7-10: Well-organized, each section earns its place, transitions are smooth

rubrics.name: Logical Coherence
rubrics.description: Do the claims and conclusions follow from the premises? Are there contradictions, logical leaps, or non-sequiturs?

scoring_guide.0-3: Multiple contradictions or non-sequiturs that undermine the argument
scoring_guide.4-6: Generally logical but with at least one noticeable gap or inconsistency
scoring_guide.7-10: Reasoning is internally consistent and conclusions follow from evidence

rubrics.name: Evidence & Support
rubrics.description: Are claims backed by examples, data, citations, or concrete detail? Or are assertions left unsupported?

scoring_guide.0-3: Assertions with no supporting evidence or examples whatsoever
scoring_guide.4-6: Some evidence present but major claims left unsupported
scoring_guide.7-10: Claims are consistently backed by relevant, specific evidence

rubrics.name: Depth of Analysis
rubrics.description: Does the piece go beyond surface-level observations? Does it engage with complexity, nuance, or trade-offs?

scoring_guide.0-3: Stays entirely at surface level, no engagement with nuance or complexity
scoring_guide.4-6: Some depth in places but misses significant complexity or counterarguments
scoring_guide.7-10: Engages seriously with complexity, explores implications, considers multiple angles

rubrics.name: Relevance & Focus
rubrics.description: Does the content stay on-topic? Is there unnecessary padding, filler, or tangential material?

scoring_guide.0-3: Frequently wanders off-topic, significant padding or filler present
scoring_guide.4-6: Mostly focused but includes some tangential or redundant material
scoring_guide.7-10: Tightly focused, every sentence earns its place, no wasted space

rubrics.name: Originality & Creativity
rubrics.description: Does the piece offer a fresh perspective, novel framing, or creative insight? Or does it repeat conventional wisdom?

scoring_guide.0-3: Entirely conventional, no original thought or fresh angle
scoring_guide.4-6: Mostly familiar territory with occasional fresh observation
scoring_guide.7-10: Genuinely novel framing or insight that adds value beyond existing discourse

rubrics.name: Actionability
rubrics.description: If the piece makes recommendations, are they specific enough to act on? Does the reader know what to do next?

scoring_guide.0-3: Recommendations are vague, absent, or impossible to act on
scoring_guide.4-6: Some actionable items but others remain too abstract or ambiguous
scoring_guide.7-10: Clear, specific next steps that a reader can immediately implement

rubrics.name: Completeness
rubrics.description: Does the piece cover all important aspects of its stated topic? Are obvious gaps or omissions present?

scoring_guide.0-3: Significant gaps leave key questions unanswered or important aspects untouched
scoring_guide.4-6: Covers the main points but misses secondary important aspects
scoring_guide.7-10: Comprehensive coverage given the stated scope, no important omissions

rubrics.name: Conciseness
rubrics.description: Is the piece as short as it can be while still being complete? Is there word inflation, redundancy, or filler?

scoring_guide.0-3: Highly verbose, could be cut by 50%+ without losing meaningful content
scoring_guide.4-6: Some redundancy or filler but not egregiously wordy
scoring_guide.7-10: Tight writing, no wasted words, every sentence adds something

rubrics.name: Technical Accuracy
rubrics.description: For pieces making factual or technical claims: are those claims correct and appropriately caveated?

scoring_guide.0-3: Contains significant factual errors that would mislead the reader
scoring_guide.4-6: Mostly accurate but with noticeable errors or harmful oversimplifications
scoring_guide.7-10: Technically sound, claims are accurate and caveated where appropriate

rubrics.name: Professional Tone
rubrics.description: Is the register appropriate for the context? Does the tone match the intended audience and purpose?

scoring_guide.0-3: Tone is clearly mismatched for context (too casual, too aggressive, condescending, etc.)
scoring_guide.4-6: Tone is mostly appropriate with occasional lapses
scoring_guide.7-10: Tone is consistently appropriate and well-calibrated to the audience
